# tg_container_state (Resource)

Manage the enabled state of a container. This resource allows you to control whether a container is running or stopped, independent of the container configuration.

## Example Usage

### Enable a container on a node

```terraform
resource "tg_container_state" "example" {
  node_id      = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001"
  container_id = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000002"
  enabled      = true
}
```

### Disable a container on a cluster

```terraform
resource "tg_container_state" "cluster_example" {
  cluster_fqdn = "my-cluster.example.com"
  container_id = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000002"
  enabled      = false
}
```

## Common Use Cases

- **Maintenance windows**: Disable a container while performing maintenance, then re-enable it when done.
- **Staged rollouts**: Control which containers are running across nodes or clusters.
- **Cost management**: Stop containers that are not needed outside of business hours.

## Behavior Notes

- On create and update, this resource reads the current container configuration from the API, sets the `enabled` field, and writes it back.
- On delete, this resource is removed from Terraform state only. The container remains in whatever state it was in when the resource was deleted.
- The `enabled` attribute is always reconciled with the API on each `terraform plan`/`apply`.
- If the same container is also managed by `tg_container`, configure `lifecycle { ignore_changes = [enabled] }` on that `tg_container` resource so `tg_container_state` can own the runtime enabled state without fighting refresh drift.

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `container_id` (String) Container ID - the UUID of the container to manage
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the container should be enabled (running) or disabled (stopped)

### Optional

- `cluster_fqdn` (String) Cluster FQDN - the fully qualified domain name of the cluster where the container runs
- `node_id` (String) Node ID - the UUID of the node where the container runs

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

## Import

Import using node ID:

```shell
terraform import tg_container_state.example 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000002
```

Import using cluster FQDN:

```shell
terraform import tg_container_state.example my-cluster.example.com/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000002
```
